_comment: Shared cross-tier wire-protocol goldens (issue #178, layout pinned by server wire codec #166). Both tiers assert this ONE file: server/wire/crosstier_wire_test.go proves the shipped Go codec encodes these values to exactly these bytes (and decodes them back), and client/tests/wire_codec_test.gd proves the GDScript decoder reads the same bytes to the same values — so neither tier can drift from the pinned layout without both tests moving together. Positions and radii are integer millimetres (int64, two's complement on the wire); tick/observer/id are unsigned 64-bit. Keep every value within +/-2^53 so JSON doubles carry it exactly on the GDScript side.
_stream_comment: Cross-tier STREAM golden (issue #198): the frame sequence a client receives across a join snapshot, per-tick deltas and a mid-stream resync, produced by the shipped tracker + encoder from the scripted scenario in server/wire/crosstier_stream_test.go (which asserts these exact bytes and that end_state is the server's authoritative view). The client replica store test folds these frames and must land on end_state exactly. Unlike the message goldens above, these frames COMPOSE as a valid stream: entered ids are absent, moved/left ids are present, ticks strictly ascend, and the second snapshot is the resync path. Regenerate only as a deliberate reviewed act: WAR_RECORD_STREAM=1 go test ./wire/ -run TestCrossTierStreamFixture -v.
